title: $disable
text: $disable Disables a menu item in a menu object. $disable set | reset $disable Return Values Table 1. Value Meaning 0 Menu item is selectable 1 Menu item is currently not selectable If an error occurs, $procerror contains the exact error. Use Use only in the preDisplay trigger of Menu objects. Description The function $disable is used to disable (or enable) a menu item Set the value to 1 to disable the menu item so it cannot be selected; the menu accelerator key is also disabled. the disabled menu item is dimmed when the menu is displayed. Set the value to 0 to enable the menu item. You can also use $disable as the target in the left side of an assignment. For example: $disable=!$disable Copy Since $disable is essentially a Boolean function, when Expression evaluates to a non-zero value, $disable becomes 1. Example: Using $disable The following example disables a menu item: trigger preDisplay set $disable end; preDisplay Copy Related Links reset Reset the value of the specified ProcScript function to 0. set Set the value of the specified ProcScript function to 1 (TRUE). $check Return or set the checked status of a menu item. $hide Return or set the display status of a menu item. Option: Copy Description and Comments Determines whether the new implementation should use the description and comments defined in the component's specification. trigger preDisplay System trigger that is executed before a menu or menu item is displayed. trigger option Trigger for handling the processing that should occur after a menu item is selected.
